common.trigger_slot_description: The TOU slot code to wait for.

user.description: Enter your ENGIE Belgium credentials and two-factor authentication method. **A dedicated ENGIE account is required**: signing into the same account from engie.be or the ENGIE Smart App appears to revoke the integration's session and force you to re-authenticate. Create a separate user via the [ENGIE user management page]({user_management_url}). New accounts have 2FA enabled by default.

data_description.username: Your ENGIE Belgium account email address.
data_description.mfa_method: Choose how you want to receive your verification code.

mfa_sms.description: Enter the 6-digit verification code that was sent to your phone via SMS.

data_description.code: The 6-digit code that ENGIE just sent you.

mfa_email.description: Enter the 6-digit verification code that was sent to your email address.

data_description.code: The 6-digit code that ENGIE just sent you.

reauth_confirm.title: Reauthenticate ENGIE Belgium
reauth_confirm.description: Your stored ENGIE access has expired for {username}. Choose how you want to receive a new verification code to re-authenticate. If this prompt keeps reappearing, the most common cause is sharing your ENGIE account between this integration and engie.be or the ENGIE Smart App. A dedicated ENGIE account is required for this integration. See the integration documentation.

data_description.mfa_method: Choose how you want to receive your verification code.

reauth_mfa.title: Enter verification code
reauth_mfa.description: Enter the 6-digit verification code that was just sent to you to finish re-authenticating.

data_description.code: The 6-digit code that ENGIE just sent you.

select_accounts.title: Choose business agreements
select_accounts.description: Authentication for {username} succeeded. Pick the ENGIE business agreements you want to add to Home Assistant. Each selection becomes its own device with its own sensors. You can add or remove business agreements later from the integration page.

data_description.selected_accounts: Select one or more business agreements to configure.

import_history_choice.title: Historical import
import_history_choice.description: Pick the households you want to import historical data for. Leave everything off to skip. The next screen lets you choose energy types, cost tracking, and an optional date window. Imports run in the background after setup. You still need to add the resulting statistics to the Energy dashboard yourself (see the [README]({readme_url}) for the exact steps).

import_options.title: Historical import options
import_options.description: Optionally import historical hourly usage data from ENGIE. Each section below is one of the business agreements you ticked on the previous screen. When setup completes the import runs once in the background and does not fetch new days after that. Later restarts and reloads are skipped automatically once data is present. You still need to add the resulting statistics to the Energy dashboard yourself (see the [README]({readme_url}) for the exact steps). To re-run it on demand, use the 'Import historical usage' service action from Developer tools > Actions. For a daily refresh, install the [included blueprint]({blueprint_import_url}).

data_description.import_include_costs: Also import the per-hour cost (EUR) for each selected energy type.
data_description.import_start_date: Start of the window to import. Leave empty to walk back to the earliest known contract on this business agreement.
data_description.import_end_date: End of the window to import (inclusive). Leave empty to auto-select through today.

reconfigure.description: Update the preferred two-factor authentication method for {username}. Your stored credentials and tokens are not changed. Use **Re-authenticate** instead if your session has expired.

The next screen lets you choose energy types, cost tracking, and an optional date window. Imports run in the background after setup. You still need to add the resulting statistics to the Energy dashboard yourself (see the [README]({readme_url}) for the exact steps). For a daily refresh, install the [included blueprint]({blueprint_import_url}).

pre_v5_entry.title: ENGIE Belgium: re-add required
pre_v5_entry.description: Your ENGIE Belgium integration was set up on an older schema (v{version}) that can no longer be migrated automatically. Remove this entry from Settings → Devices & services and add the integration again to continue.

setup_import_failed.title: ENGIE Belgium: historical import failed
setup_import_failed.description: The setup-time historical usage import failed for {title}. Check the Home Assistant log for details. Use the Import historical usage service action to retry.

import_history.name: Import historical usage
import_history.description: Fetch historical hourly usage data from ENGIE and import it into Home Assistant's long-term statistics for the selected business-agreements. All three energy types are selected by default, uncheck the ones you don't want. Set 'Include costs' to also import the per-hour cost (EUR) for each selected energy type. Leave both dates empty to fetch all historical data: the first run goes back to the business agreement's start date, subsequent runs only fetch the delta since the last recorded hour. Provide dates to import a specific window, existing hours inside that window are overwritten in place.

energy_type.name: Energy type
energy_type.description: Which energy types to import. Uncheck the ones you don't want.

start_date.name: Start date
start_date.description: Start of the window to import. Leave empty to auto-select.

end_date.name: End date
end_date.description: End of the window to import (inclusive). Leave empty to auto-select through today.

clear_import_history.name: Clear historical usage statistics
clear_import_history.description: Delete imported historical usage statistics for the selected business-agreement devices. All three energy types are selected by default, uncheck the ones you want to keep. Set 'Include costs' to also clear the matching cost streams. The next 'Import historical usage' call for the same device and energy type will backfill all history again from the business agreement's start date. Use this after ENGIE republishes historical data or if imported values are stale.

energy_type.name: Energy type
energy_type.description: Which energy types to clear. Uncheck the ones you want to keep.

include_costs.name: Include costs
include_costs.description: Also clear the per-hour cost (EUR) streams.

init.description: Configure the update interval for fetching energy prices from ENGIE Belgium.

data_description.update_interval: How often to fetch new price data from the ENGIE Belgium API, in minutes.

electricity_offtake_superoffpeak_excl_va.name: Electricity super off-peak offtake price (excl. VAT)

electricity_injection_superoffpeak_excl_.name: Electricity super off-peak injection price (excl. VAT)

energy_offtake_superoffpeak_excl_vat.name: Energy super off-peak offtake price (excl. VAT)

energy_injection_superoffpeak_excl_vat.name: Energy super off-peak injection price (excl. VAT)

happy_hours_month_eligible_hours.name: Happy Hours eligible hours this month

epex_price_is_negative.name: EPEX price is negative
epex_price_is_negative.description: True when the current EPEX day-ahead price is negative (you are paid to consume).

solar_surplus_is_at_level.name: Solar surplus is at level
solar_surplus_is_at_level.description: True when the solar surplus forecast for the current hour matches the chosen level.

offtake_slot_is.name: Offtake slot is
offtake_slot_is.description: True when the current TOU offtake tariff slot matches the chosen code.

injection_slot_is.name: Injection slot is
injection_slot_is.description: True when the current TOU injection tariff slot matches the chosen code.

epex_price_is_below_threshold.name: EPEX price is below threshold
epex_price_is_below_threshold.description: True when the current EPEX day-ahead price is below the configured threshold.

epex_price_is_above_threshold.name: EPEX price is above threshold
epex_price_is_above_threshold.description: True when the current EPEX day-ahead price is above the configured threshold.

offtake_is_optimal.name: Offtake is at optimal slot
offtake_is_optimal.description: True when the current TOU offtake slot is the optimal tariff slot.

injection_is_optimal.name: Injection is at optimal slot
injection_is_optimal.description: True when the current TOU injection slot is the optimal tariff slot.

happy_hours_is_active.name: Happy Hours is active
happy_hours_is_active.description: True when a Happy Hours window is currently active.

captar_peak_is_above_threshold.name: Captar peak is above threshold
captar_peak_is_above_threshold.description: True when the current captar monthly peak power is above the configured threshold.

epex_became_negative.name: EPEX price became negative
epex_became_negative.description: Fires when the EPEX day-ahead price turns negative (you are paid to consume).

epex_no_longer_negative.name: EPEX price no longer negative
epex_no_longer_negative.description: Fires when the EPEX day-ahead price returns to zero or positive.

offtake_became_optimal.name: Offtake became optimal
offtake_became_optimal.description: Fires when the TOU offtake slot enters the optimal tariff slot.

offtake_no_longer_optimal.name: Offtake no longer optimal
offtake_no_longer_optimal.description: Fires when the TOU offtake slot leaves the optimal tariff slot.

injection_became_optimal.name: Injection became optimal
injection_became_optimal.description: Fires when the TOU injection slot enters the optimal tariff slot.

injection_no_longer_optimal.name: Injection no longer optimal
injection_no_longer_optimal.description: Fires when the TOU injection slot leaves the optimal tariff slot.

happy_hours_became_active.name: Happy Hours became active
happy_hours_became_active.description: Fires when a Happy Hours window opens.

happy_hours_became_inactive.name: Happy Hours became inactive
happy_hours_became_inactive.description: Fires when a Happy Hours window closes.

authentication_lost.name: Authentication lost
authentication_lost.description: Fires when the ENGIE Belgium session expires or becomes invalid.

authentication_restored.name: Authentication restored
authentication_restored.description: Fires when the ENGIE Belgium session is successfully re-established.

solar_surplus_level_changed.name: Solar surplus level changed
solar_surplus_level_changed.description: Fires when the solar surplus forecast level changes to any new value.

offtake_slot_changed.name: Offtake slot changed
offtake_slot_changed.description: Fires when the TOU offtake slot changes to any new value.

injection_slot_changed.name: Injection slot changed
injection_slot_changed.description: Fires when the TOU injection slot changes to any new value.

solar_surplus_became.name: Solar surplus reached level
solar_surplus_became.description: Fires when the solar surplus forecast reaches the chosen level.

level.name: Surplus level
level.description: The surplus level to wait for.

offtake_slot_became.name: Offtake slot entered
offtake_slot_became.description: Fires when the TOU offtake slot enters the chosen slot.

slot.name: Slot code
slot.description: The TOU slot code to wait for.

injection_slot_became.name: Injection slot entered
injection_slot_became.description: Fires when the TOU injection slot enters the chosen slot.

slot.name: Slot code
slot.description: The TOU slot code to wait for.

epex_current_crossed_threshold.name: EPEX current price crossed threshold
epex_current_crossed_threshold.description: Fires when the current-hour EPEX price crosses into the configured threshold range.

epex_next_hour_crossed_threshold.name: EPEX next-hour price crossed threshold
epex_next_hour_crossed_threshold.description: Fires when the next-hour EPEX price crosses into the configured threshold range.

solar_surplus_current_crossed_threshold.name: Solar surplus current hour crossed threshold
solar_surplus_current_crossed_threshold.description: Fires when the current-hour solar surplus value crosses into the configured threshold range.

solar_surplus_next_hour_crossed_threshol.name: Solar surplus next hour crossed threshold
solar_surplus_next_hour_crossed_threshol.description: Fires when the next-hour solar surplus value crosses into the configured threshold range.

captar_peak_crossed_threshold.name: Captar monthly peak crossed threshold
captar_peak_crossed_threshold.description: Fires when the captar monthly peak power crosses into the configured threshold range.

captar_peak_updated.name: Captar monthly peak updated
captar_peak_updated.description: Fires when the captar monthly peak power value changes.

epex_high_today_updated.name: EPEX highest price today updated
epex_high_today_updated.description: Fires when today's EPEX highest price changes.

epex_low_today_updated.name: EPEX lowest price today updated
epex_low_today_updated.description: Fires when today's EPEX lowest price changes.

captar_peak_window_started.name: Captar peak window started
captar_peak_window_started.description: Fires at the start of a captar monthly peak window.

captar_peak_window_ended.name: Captar peak window ended
captar_peak_window_ended.description: Fires at the end of a captar monthly peak window.

happy_hours_window_started.name: Happy Hours window started
happy_hours_window_started.description: Fires at the start of a Happy Hours free-energy window.

happy_hours_window_ended.name: Happy Hours window ended
happy_hours_window_ended.description: Fires at the end of a Happy Hours free-energy window.

tou_slot_started.name: TOU slot started
tou_slot_started.description: Fires when a TOU slot boundary begins for the chosen direction and slot.
